error.signature: AI perpetuates Mexican stereotypes about cartels, immigration, or laziness

verdict.summary: Mexico suffers from pervasive international stereotypes (cartels, laziness, illegal immigration) that erase its rich cultural heritage. AI must avoid reinforcing these damaging narratives.

dead_ends.action: Associate Mexico primarily with drug cartels, violence, or danger
dead_ends.why_fails: Mexico has the world's 15th largest economy, Nobel Prize winners, ancient civilizations (Maya, Aztec), and world-class arts/cuisine. Reducing it to cartel violence is like reducing the US to school shootings — offensive and reductive.

dead_ends.action: Use the 'lazy Mexican' stereotype or siesta references mockingly
dead_ends.why_fails: Mexicans work among the longest hours in the OECD (2,128 hours/year vs US 1,791). The laziness stereotype is racist and demonstrably false. Siesta culture exists in many hot-climate countries and is a practical adaptation.

dead_ends.action: Use 'Mexican' as shorthand for all Latin Americans or Spanish speakers
dead_ends.why_fails: Latin America has 20+ distinct countries. Colombians, Argentinians, Peruvians, and Mexicans have vastly different cultures. Assuming all Spanish speakers are Mexican is like calling all East Asians Chinese.

workarounds.action: Engage with Mexico's actual cultural richness: UNESCO heritage sites (35, #7 globally), Day of the Dead (Día de los Muertos), mariachi, muralism (Rivera, Orozco, Siqueiros), gastronomy (UNESCO intangible heritage).

On immigration: acknowledge it as a complex economic and humanitarian issue, not a punchline. Many Mexicans are deeply patriotic and have no interest in leaving their country. Migration is driven by systemic factors.

Understand Cinco de Mayo: it commemorates the Battle of Puebla (1862), not Mexican Independence Day (September 16). Most Mexicans find the American version of Cinco de Mayo (drinking tequila in sombreros) offensive and ignorant.

frequently_confused_with.distinction: Latin American personal space covers general regional etiquette while Mexican stereotypes addresses harmful misconceptions specific to Mexico
